title: CenterPoint Energy to Invest in Renewable Natural Gas Created from Twin Citiesâ€™ Metro Food and Yard Waste
content: CenterPoint Energy has signed an agreement with Dem-Con HZI Bioenergy, LLC (DCHZI), to purchase renewable natural gas (RNG) created from Twin Citiesâ€™ area food and yard waste. This made-in-Minnesota RNG is expected to connect to CenterPointâ€™s distribution system in Shakopee, Minn. in 2027, and provide customers with renewable energy for their homes and businesses. DCHZIâ€™s facility in Shakopee is expected to be in service in 2027 and will produce the RNG from local waste products collected from Twin Cities metro residents in an oxygen-free tank, through a process known as anaerobic digestion.
author: Analysts Account
